title: Waiting for my love to come home
7 of Cups
medium: Acrylic, ink, marker, house paint on canvas.
description: this is a painting about illusions.

title: Eve
description: Three persimmons rest on a rumpled linen cloth, their translucent orange skins catching afternoon light from an unseen window. A quiet meditation on patience and ripeness.

title: A Vulnerable Portrait of a Serial Optimist
medium: Acrylic, Oil & Gold Leaf on Canvas

title: seek & destroy
medium: acrylic, graffiti spray paint & oil pastel on canvas.
description: A lot of the floral elements within my personal work are derived from very few memories i have of my childhood, a lot of which i’ve repressed over the years. i have very distinct memories of the house i grew up in having a huge flower garden. as a child most of my artwork involved flowers, dogs & cats, and humans scribbled in a stick figuresque fashion. my inner child paintings aren’t specifically supposed to be "good", they are the abstract scribbles that support the soft, white underbelly of my surrealist and impressionist paintings. one cannot exist without the other. seek & destroy is about ruining reputations and definitions and expectations. a big part of my childhood was being a put together adult. read that again. my adulthood is about being a child again.

title: February Table
description: A half-eaten pomegranate, a ceramic mug of cold tea, and a dog-eared paperback share a weathered oak table. Morning light pools on the wood grain in soft geometric shapes.

title: An abstract expression of Yin & Yang - Masculine & Feminine energies
The Flower That Blooms in a Dark Room
description: 1/2 of my commissions of Cancer Research UK.

title: Forcing Orchids
description: Forcing orchids is my first abstract work of the year, intended to be created in one sitting without any expectations on the work.

title: Jama
